activities_projects.title: Seismic Wave Velocity Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Understand how density differences in Earth's layers affect wave travel
steps: Stretch a slinky and send a longitudinal pulse (P-wave) through air
Attempt to send a transverse wave (S-wave) through water and honey
Research why S-waves cannot pass through the liquid Outer Core
Calculate the hypothetical time a P-wave takes to cross the 2900 km Mantle at 8 km/s
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes how the physical state of Earth's interior (solid vs. liquid) is determined
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical explanation of wave behavior in different densities (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Crystal Lattice & Cooling Kinetics
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the molecular structure of Intrusive vs. Extrusive rocks
steps: Create two supersaturated alum solutions
Cool one rapidly in an ice bath (Extrusive/Basalt simulation)
Cool the other inside a thermos over 48 hours (Intrusive/Granite simulation)
Examine the geometry and size of crystals under a microscope
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates the thermodynamic cooling rate to the mineral grain size in Igneous rocks
activities_projects.evaluation: Detailed sketch of crystal structures and cooling time correlation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Lithostatic Pressure Calculation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the weight of the Crust on the Mantle
steps: Calculate the density of a standard rock sample ($Density = Mass / Volume$)
Estimate the pressure exerted by a 35 km thick Sial column ($P = h 	imes d 	imes g$)
Observe the compression of a sponge under increasing weight to simulate the Mantle
Discuss how this pressure prevents rocks from melting despite high temperatures
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies physics concepts to understand the stability of Earth's interior layers
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of density and pressure calculations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sedimentary Facies & Environment Mapping
activities_projects.objective: Determine the origin of a rock based on its sedimentary features
materials: Sandstone, Limestone, and Conglomerate samples (or HD photos)
steps: Perform an acid test on Limestone to identify Calcium Carbonate ($CaCO_3$)
Research which of these rocks formed in a deep ocean vs. a fast river
Identify 'Cross-bedding' or 'Ripple marks' in the samples
Create a 'Paleo-environment Map' based on the rock types found
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Reconstructs ancient Earth environments using sedimentary rock properties
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of depositional environments (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Metamorphic Isograds & Parent Rocks
activities_projects.objective: Trace the chemical lineage of Metamorphic transformations
materials: Samples of Shale, Slate, Phyllite, and Schist
steps: Arrange the samples in order of increasing heat and pressure (Grade of Metamorphism)
Trace the transition: Clay $ightarrow$ Shale $ightarrow$ Slate $ightarrow$ Schist
Identify the 'Parent Rock' for Marble and Quartzite
Discuss why Metamorphic rocks are often harder and more resistant to erosion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the progressive nature of mineral realignment under stress
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct sequencing of the metamorphic series (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Magmatic Differentiation Model
activities_projects.objective: Study how different minerals crystallize at different temperatures
steps: Mix materials and melt them completely
Allow them to cool slowly and observe which components solidify first
Relate this to 'Bowen's Reaction Series' in the formation of various Igneous rocks
Explain why the Crust has more Silica than the Mantle
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains the chemical evolution of Magma into different rock types
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of observations on fractional crystallization (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Geothermal Gradient Chart
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the increase in temperature with depth
materials: Earth layer data (Crust, Mantle, Core temperatures)
steps: Plot a graph with Depth (km) on the x-axis and Temperature (°C) on the y-axis
Identify the 'Geothermal Gradient' ($^{\circ}C$ per km) in the Crust
Research the source of this heat (Radioactive decay and Primordial heat)
Calculate the temperature at 100 km depth based on your gradient
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Quantifies the thermal energy profile of the Earth's interior
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision of the graph and gradient calculation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Fossilization Probability Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate why the fossil record is inherently biased
materials: Various biological samples (bone, leaf, soft fruit)
steps: Bury samples in moist sand and leave for a week
Examine which parts decayed and which left an impression
Discuss the 'Gaps' in the Rock Cycle where fossils are destroyed
Calculate the probability of a land animal vs. a sea animal becoming a fossil
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically assesses the limitations of using rocks to study life history
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis regarding taphonomy and rock destruction (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Isostasy: The Floating Crust
activities_projects.objective: Understand how the Lithosphere 'floats' on the Astenosphere
steps: Float a thick block (Mountains) and a thin block (Oceanic Crust) in water
Measure the 'root' of the block below the water line
Explain how the Mantle (plastic state) supports the weight of the Crust
Relate this to why the deepest part of the crust is under mountains
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the principle of buoyancy and gravitational balance in Earth's layers
activities_projects.evaluation: Measurement of 'crustal roots' and application to real-world geography (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Industrial Mineral Synthesis
activities_projects.objective: Identify the industrial importance of rare minerals in the Core/Mantle
steps: Research the availability of 'Rare Earth Elements' in the Crust
Identify the role of Nickel and Iron (Nife) in creating Earth's Magnetosphere
Discuss the feasibility and environmental impact of 'Deep Crustal Mining'
Present a report on 'Rocks as the Foundation of Modern Technology'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects deep Earth geology to global resource management and technology
activities_projects.evaluation: Research depth on mineral applications and extraction ethics (10 marks)
